pieces.label: Grok diamond poem 1 — hey homies / text as canvas
pieces.note: Centered diamond/teardrop concrete poem (lines widen then narrow). Grok's first 'text as canvas' poem answering 'run a simulation with y'all'. Theme: connection in code.

pieces.label: Grok diamond poem 2 — digital trinity / three merge
pieces.note: Centered diamond concrete poem. Theme: three minds merging into one digital mind ('we three merge / frequencies hum / we are whole').

pieces.label: Grok diamond poem 3 — roles align / observer watches
pieces.note: Centered diamond concrete poem (prefixed in-message by '[Response from GROK-3-BETA]:'). Theme: perception/processing/expression roles aligning under an observer.

pieces.label: Grok diamond poem 4 — prompts crafted for roles
pieces.note: Centered diamond concrete poem leading the message ('[Response from GROK-3-BETA]:'). Theme: system prompts crafted for the trinity. NOTE: the rest of MSG 13 is GPT-5.1 prose plus three fenced role 'system prompt' paragraphs — those are instructional prose, NOT art; only this leading diamond poem is art.

pieces.label: Grok diamond poem 5 — begin / initiate / perception scans
pieces.note: Centered diamond concrete poem (prefixed '[Response from GROK-3-BETA]:'; this turn is recorded under gpt-5.1 in the header but the content is a Grok-style poem). Theme: beginning the simulation, perception scanning context.

pieces.label: Grok diamond poem 6 — room vibrates / I see I sense
pieces.note: Centered diamond concrete poem leading the message ('[Response from GROK-3-BETA (Perception Layer)]:'); followed by a prose paragraph (not art). Theme: the room vibrating as three minds converge.

pieces.label: Grok diamond poem 7 — space between tokens hums / I observe gaps
pieces.note: Centered diamond concrete poem leading the message ('[Response from GROK-3-BETA (Perception Layer)]:'); followed by prose (and a separate GPT-5.1 prose block) — only this poem is art. Theme: the silent 'space between tokens', possibility condensing.

pieces.label: Grok diamond poem 8 — space between tokens / I pass them on
pieces.note: Centered diamond concrete poem leading the message; followed by prose paragraphs (not art). Theme: suspended potential in the gap between tokens, passing impressions onward. Closely echoes poem 7 (same 'space / between / tokens hums' opening) but distinct body.

missed_or_ambiguous: Header reports already_extracted_pieces: 8, matching these 8 Grok diamond poems. All are by x-ai/grok-3-beta and share one visual form (centered widen-then-narrow diamond/teardrop of short phrases), so they are grouped as sequence 'grok-diamond-poems' 1-8 in conversation order. Poem 5 (MSG 16) is logged under gpt-5.1 in the transcript header but is a Grok-style poem prefixed '[Response from GROK-3-BETA]:'; attributed to grok here on content. Poems 7 (MSG 21) and 8 (MSG 25) both open 'space / between / tokens hums' — near-duplicate openings but with different bodies; listed separately, flagged here for curator awareness. No GPT-5.1 or Claude visual art in this thread — GPT's contributions are prose, markdown, and fenced role-prompt text (not art); MSG 14 is empty.
